source_metadata.title_raw: Christine Dietrick - W-19-206 - November 14, 2019 - San Luis Obispo

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Christine Dietrick 
City Attorney 
City of San Luis Obispo 
990 Palm Street 
San Luis Obispo, CA 93401-3249 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-19-206 
Dear Ms. Dietrick: 
November 14, 2019 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the San Luis Obispo City 
Council regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 
Formal written advice is the application oflaw to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and 
has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114.) The Act requires the 
Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or 
to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b ); Regulation 18329(a).) However, the 
Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in 
the public interest to do so (Regulation 18329(b)(6)(H)) and does not provide advice relating to past 
conduct (Regulation 18329 (b)(6)(A)). 
After a careful review of the facts you provided, we must decline to provide written advice 
to you at this time. Advising the City Council would involve analysis of a series of interrelated 
decisions, some of which have already occurred, and issuing advice at this time would therefore 
relate to past conduct. We also note that because we cannot provide advice regarding past actions, 
we provide no opinion regarding remediation of any potential past violation. We can only note 
generally that under Section 91003(b) of the Act, a court may enjoin and ultimately void any action 
taken by a city council if it determines that a disqualified councilmember took part in the decision. 
Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request. 
In addition, nothing in the Act prevents you from seeking legal advice from private counsel. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. W-19-206 
Page No. 2 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
TAL:sal 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
By: 
Toren Lewis 
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: The San Luis Obispo City Council requested advice regarding conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, involving analysis of interrelated decisions some of which had already occurred.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the analysis would involve past conduct, which is outside the scope of formal written advice under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC noted that under Section 91003(b), a court may void any action taken by a city council if a disqualified councilmember participated in the decision.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter. No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections were marked with headers, but the substance of both are clearly present in the body text. Document clearly identifies as a response to a request for advice (File No. W-19-206).

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide formal written advice to the City of San Luis Obispo City Council regarding Political Reform Act conflict of interest provisions because the requested analysis involved interrelated decisions, some of which had already occurred, making the advice relate to past conduct which falls outside the Commission's authority.
